assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 6-13: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed version = 0.11.14. All four required fields are explicitly present and correct.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence:
data-extraction.md lines 28-33: '## Ecosystem Detection

The vulnerable library is **quinn-proto**, a Rust crate. Ecosystem: **Cargo**.' Reasoning is explicitly tied to the library name (quinn-proto as a Rust crate).

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 7-8 lists all versions in both streams: '2.1.0, 2.1.1' for stream 2.1.x and '2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4' for stream 2.2.x. The version impact table (lines 14-22) includes all 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 — matching the matrix exactly with no versions skipped.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Source Tag column shows pinned tags for each version: v0.3.8, v0.3.12, v0.4.5, v0.4.8, v0.4.9, v0.4.11, v0.4.12. affects-versions.md line 31 also says 'Lock file analysis at pinned source commits from security-matrix.md shows:' and lists each version with its pinned tag (e.g., 'RHTPA 2.2.0 (v0.4.5): quinn-proto 0.11.9').

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 20: '| 2.2.2 | 2.2.x | v0.4.9 | -- | YES | backend retag of 2.2.1 (same as 2.2.1) |'. Version 2.2.2 is identified as a retag of 2.2.1, the quinn-proto column shows '--' (no independent lock file entry), and Affected? is YES, carrying forward the affected status from 2.2.1.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence:
affects-versions.md lines 25-27 shows an explicit diff block: 'Current:  [RHTPA 2.0.0]
Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]' with rationale citing lock file analysis at pinned commits. The diff is clearly shown.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses version names 'RHTPA 2.2.0', 'RHTPA 2.2.1', 'RHTPA 2.2.2' throughout. The proposed Jira mutation block (lines 43-49) uses placeholder text '<RHTPA-2.2.0-jira-id>' (not hardcoded numeric IDs), and line 51 notes 'Jira version IDs would be discovered dynamically via getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields'. Version names from the matrix are used, not hardcoded IDs.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence:
remediation.md Task 1 (lines 29-75): includes '## Repository
rhtpa-backend', '## Target Branch
release/0.4.z', '## Acceptance Criteria' with checklist items. Task 2 (lines 82-133): includes Repository, Target Branch, Acceptance Criteria, and Dependencies section stating 'Depends on: [upstream-backport-task-key] (upstream backport must merge first)'. Linkage between tasks is shown via '- Link to upstream backport task with type "Blocks" (upstream blocks downstream)'.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 12-13: 'Cargo (source dependency) -- requires two tasks per stream: an upstream backport task and a downstream propagation subtask.' Task 1 is 'Upstream Backport (source repo fix)' and Task 2 is 'Downstream Propagation (Konflux release repo update)'. Line 133: 'Link to upstream backport task with type "Blocks" (upstream blocks downstream)', confirming the Blocks dependency between the two tasks.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md line 39: 'Proposed Jira Mutation (requires engineer confirmation)'. remediation.md line 278: 'Post-Triage Summary (proposed comment for TC-8001)'. remediation.md lines 316-320: label addition ('Add label ai-cve-triaged'), status transition ('Transition TC-8001 to In Progress'), and assignment are all listed as proposed actions within a proposed summary comment block, not as executed commands. All sections use 'Proposed' framing throughout.
